message:
test(indexer): drive ibuuh.19 structured-logs test through the public cleanup entrypoint

The regression test for the ibuuh.19 structured tracing events
was calling \`LexicalCleanupDryRunPlan::record_inventory\` directly.
That exercised the right code path but via a handle operators
never reach — \`cass diag\` and \`cass doctor\` go through
\`LexicalCleanupDryRunPlan::from_manifests\`, which loops over
manifests and calls \`record_inventory\` internally.

Switch the test to
\`LexicalCleanupDryRunPlan::from_manifests([&manifest])\` so the
subscriber captures events from the same driver operators trigger
end-to-end. If a future refactor moves the emission site from
\`record_inventory\` to a sibling helper in
\`from_manifests\`, the test still covers it; if a refactor
removes the emission entirely, the test fails on the \`assert!\`
count as before.

Drive-by: add \`Debug\` to the \`CapturedEvent\` derive so test
failures print a readable dump of the captured field map instead
of requiring a rerun under \`--nocapture\`. Also rename the bound
var to \`_plan\` to silence the \"unused\" lint without an
\`#[allow]\`.

Pure test-path refinement; no change to the production emissions
or to what the test asserts about them.
